task.title: Permanently configuring a network device to accept all traffic using nmstatectl

li.p: The enp1s0.yml file that you used to configure the device is available.

context.p: Use the nmstatectl utility to configure a device to accept all traffic regardless of the MAC addresses through the Nmstate API. The Nmstate API ensures that, after setting the configuration, the result matches the configuration file. If anything fails, nmstatectl automatically rolls back the changes to avoid leaving the system in an incorrect state.

step.cmd: Edit the existing enp1s0.yml file for the enp1s0 connection and add the following content to it:

info.codeblock:
--- interfaces: - name: enp1s0
type: ethernet state: up accept -all-mac-address: true
info.p: These settings configure the enp1s0 device to accept all traffic.

li.p: Verify that the 802-3-ethernet.accept-all-mac-addresses mode is enabled:
li.codeblock:
# nmstatectl show enp1s0
interfaces: - name: enp1s0 type: ethernet state: up accept-all-mac-addresses: true ...
li.p: The 802-3-ethernet.accept-all-mac-addresses: true indicates that the mode is enabled.
